general_warnings: Top-level objective/G/P and verification fields prove feasibility and objective recomputation for the reported route, not necessarily optimality for the original problem.
The meaning of lower_bound/upper_bound/gap is method-specific. For diagnostics these fields may refer to a fixed-cap surrogate, pricing cost, LP relaxation, or restricted pool.
status=optimal is a global original-problem certificate only for methods whose certificate conditions close the original objective.

method_scopes.solves_original_objective: yes, only when the full frontier is certified and status is optimal
method_scopes.uses_restricted_column_or_path_pool: uses restricted masters internally, but exact pricing closure removes pool restriction for certified intervals
method_scopes.status_optimal_scope: global original EBRP certificate only when full frontier conditions hold
certificate_conditions: frontier covers G in [0, incumbent objective]
each interval is closed or bound-fathomed by a valid lower bound
aggregated lower bound reaches incumbent objective
exact pricing closes every relevant branch-price node
independent verifier confirms incumbent feasibility and objective
fields_proving_conditions: notes: frontier certification summary with unresolved_intervals=0 and invalid_bound_intervals=0

method_scopes.uses_restricted_column_or_path_pool: not for current compact-fallback optimal reports; full route-load enumeration exists separately and must complete to be global
method_scopes.status_optimal_scope: global original EBRP certificate when certificate indicates complete exact search or strengthened compact CPLEX optimality
certificate_conditions: complete route-load enumeration plus exact master, or complete inventory-route proof, or strengthened compact CPLEX reports optimality
fields_proving_conditions: notes: CPLEX solution status: integer optimal solution

method_scopes.status_optimal_scope: global original EBRP certificate when CPLEX optimality and verifier both hold
certificate_conditions: compact strengthened MILP is solved to CPLEX optimality
fields_proving_conditions: notes: CPLEX compact MILP with operation-time conservation, mode constraints, subset duration cuts, and symmetry cuts
notes: CPLEX solution status: integer optimal solution

aliases: plain CPLEX when notes say original-style Gini product linearization
method_scopes.status_optimal_scope: global original EBRP certificate only when status is optimal
certificate_conditions: CPLEX reports optimality or closed MIP gap

method_scopes.solves_only_fixed_gini_cap_min_p: continuous LP relaxation of fixed-cap min P, not integer min P
method_scopes.uses_restricted_column_or_path_pool: starts restricted, but root LP is full-column only if exact pricing closes
certificate_conditions: no negative reduced-cost route-load column remains

method_scopes.solves_original_objective: no, unless wrapped by a complete gcap-frontier
method_scopes.solves_only_fixed_gini_cap_min_p: yes for fixed-cap or fixed-interval surrogate tree
method_scopes.uses_restricted_column_or_path_pool: uses restricted masters at nodes; exact pricing closure is required at each solved node
certificate_conditions: all open nodes exhausted, fathomed, infeasible, or reconstructed
exact pricing closed every relevant node
route incumbent, if used, verifies and lies in requested true Gini interval

method_scopes.solves_only_fixed_gini_cap_min_p: no; one LP branch level only
method_scopes.uses_restricted_column_or_path_pool: restricted LPs with exact pricing closure for root and two children
yes; current pool is one-stop pickup columns
certificate_conditions: exact integer master over supplied pool exhausts
global only if supplied pool is proven to be all feasible route-load columns

method_scopes.uses_restricted_column_or_path_pool: restricted diagnostic LP, closed by exact pricing for that diagnostic model
certificate_conditions: exact pricing proves no negative reduced-cost diagnostic column
pricing enumeration/DP completes for each vehicle
violated cut found in diagnostic pool

method_scopes.solves_original_objective: yes if enumeration and exact master complete
method_scopes.uses_restricted_column_or_path_pool: no if all feasible columns are enumerated; current route-enum artifacts did not complete
method_scopes.status_optimal_scope: global only after complete enumeration plus exact master
fields_proving_conditions: certificate: all feasible route-load columns were enumerated

current_non_certificate_example.certificate: not certified; route-load enumeration did not complete

compare_v10_m2_average_csv_audit.speedup_interpretation: Do not call this a certified-optimal speedup because the CPLEX baseline did not close.
compare_v10_m2_average_csv_audit.required_wording: tailored certified within 55.77s, CPLEX did not certify within 120.16s
compare_v10_m2_average_csv_audit.reason: The plain CPLEX run has cplex_status=not_certified, cplex_lb < cplex_ub, and cplex_gap > 0 even though it found the same incumbent objective.

reporting_rules: verification.feasible and verification.objective_matches prove route feasibility and objective recomputation, not global optimality by themselves.
Global original-problem optimality also requires method-scope closure.
gap=0 in diagnostic methods is subproblem gap unless the certificate text explicitly closes the original problem.
A restricted-pool master is global only if the pool is proven to contain all feasible route-load columns.
A CPLEX incumbent equal to the tailored incumbent is not a certificate unless CPLEX also closes its lower bound.
